sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_dates (id INT, event_date DATE, event_duration INTERVAL DAY);
INSERT INTO test_dates VALUES (1, DATE '2023-01-01', INTERVAL '5 days'), (2, DATE '2023-01-10', INTERVAL '10 days'), (3, DATE '2023-01-15', INTERVAL '15 days');
SELECT max(event_date + event_duration) AS latest_event FROM test_dates;
SELECT id, event_date, event_duration, max(event_date + event_duration) OVER (PARTITION BY id) AS max_duration FROM test_dates ORDER BY max_duration DESC;
CREATE TABLE test_values (a INT, b INT);
SELECT a, b, max(COALESCE(b + 10, 0)) AS max_b_plus FROM test_values GROUP BY a HAVING max_b_plus > 100;
SELECT a, max(b + INTERVAL (10 * a) DAY) AS max_duration FROM test_values GROUP BY a ORDER BY max_duration DESC;
CREATE TABLE test_dates_extended (event_id INT, start_date DATE, duration INT);
INSERT INTO test_dates_extended VALUES (1, DATE '2023-02-01', 5), (2, DATE '2023-02-15', NULL), (3, DATE '2023-01-25', 10);
SELECT event_id, start_date + INTERVAL (COALESCE(duration, 0)) DAY AS end_date FROM test_dates_extended WHERE end_date IS NOT NULL;

WITH date_calculations AS (SELECT event_id, max(start_date + INTERVAL (duration) DAY) AS max_end_date FROM test_dates_extended GROUP BY event_id) SELECT * FROM date_calculations WHERE max_end_date > DATE '2023-02-10';
